title: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Little Italy
metaTitle: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Little Italy, Cleveland
metaDescription: Searching for 3 bedroom houses for rent in Little Italy, Cleveland? See rent ranges, nearby options, transit, and how to contact our leasing team.
primaryKeyword: 3 bedroom houses for rent Little Italy cleveland
secondaryKeywords: three bedroom homes near university circle
cleveland 3 bedroom house rent little italy
answerBox: Three-bedroom houses located directly inside Little Italy are limited, since the district is small and dense; most nearby 3-bedroom rentals sit just outside it in the broader University Circle-area market. Citywide, a 3-bedroom home in Cleveland typically rents between about $1,100 and $1,500 a month as of mid-2026.

sections.h2: Finding a 3-bedroom house near Little Italy
body: Little Italy is one of Cleveland's smallest residential districts, centered on **Mayfield Road** about five miles east of Downtown and pressed directly against **University Circle** and **Lake View Cemetery**. Its housing stock is dense and mostly pre-1920s narrow-lot homes and duplexes, so the number of standalone 3-bedroom houses available at any given time inside the district itself is small.

Renters specifically looking for three bedrooms usually find a larger selection just outside Little Italy's core, in the broader [University Circle rental market](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-university-circle-cleveland/) or further east in neighborhoods like Hough and Glenville. [Tell our leasing team you need three bedrooms](/p/book-showing) and we'll be upfront about what's realistically available near this pocket of the city.

sections.h2: What does a 3-bedroom rental cost near Little Italy?
body: There's no rent index published specifically for a district as small as Little Italy, so the most useful reference is Cleveland's citywide 3-bedroom data. Per Zumper's rent report dated July 4, 2026, the citywide median for a 3-bedroom home is **$1,350** a month, up 4.2% year-over-year. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on professionally managed apartments shows a much higher 3-bedroom average of **$2,716**, reflecting newer, larger buildings rather than the older individual houses more typical of a district like Little Italy.

Rent Finder Cleveland's own 3-bedroom rental homes across Greater Cleveland's east and southeast side typically price between the two figures above, generally closer to the Zumper end of the range than the RentCafe apartment average.

rows: Rent Finder Cleveland portfolio-wide typical range

sections.h2: Where 3-bedroom houses are easier to find near Little Italy
body: The University Circle area immediately surrounding Little Italy leans toward apartments, but standalone 3-bedroom houses are more common a bit further into Cleveland's east side. Hough, between Downtown and the Circle, and Glenville, to the northeast near St. Clair Avenue, both have a larger supply of rental houses, and both fall within [Rent Finder Cleveland's core service area](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-glenville-cleveland/).

items: **Little Italy:** Centered on Mayfield Road, borders Lake View Cemetery and University Circle
**Hough:** East side, ZIP 44103, between Downtown and University Circle
**Glenville:** Northeast side, ZIP 44108, near University Circle and St. Clair Avenue
**Distance:** Little Italy is about 5 miles east of Downtown Cleveland

body: Little Italy has some of the best direct transit access on Cleveland's East Side. The **RTA Red Line** heavy rail serves the University Circle station, connecting directly to Downtown's Tower City and on to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, while the **HealthLine** bus rapid transit runs along Euclid Avenue between Public Square and University Circle in a dedicated median busway.

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers for larger households near Little Italy
body: Households that need a 3-bedroom home to meet CMHA's voucher size standards can apply for a Housing Choice Voucher year-round through the [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority's online portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). CMHA's published income limit for a 4-person household, effective April 3, 2025, is **$49,700**; selection then runs through periodic mini-lotteries rather than a simple waiting line.

Our current rental portfolio is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast-side neighborhoods rather than directly in Little Italy, so we can't promise a specific voucher-friendly 3-bedroom home in this exact district today. Every home we manage anywhere in Greater Cleveland accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — read our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) or tell us what you need and we'll point you to comparable available homes.

faq.q: Are there 3-bedroom houses for rent in Little Italy?
faq.a: Very few are located directly inside Little Italy, since it's a small, dense district built mostly around narrow-lot homes and duplexes. Most available 3-bedroom rentals near this part of Cleveland sit just outside the district, in the broader University Circle area or neighborhoods like Hough and Glenville.

faq.q: What does a 3-bedroom house rent for near Little Italy?
faq.a: There's no rent index specific to Little Italy, but citywide, a 3-bedroom home has a median rent of about $1,350 a month per Zumper's July 2026 report, while professionally managed apartments average considerably more at about $2,716 per RentCafe's July 2026 data.

faq.q: What neighborhoods near Little Italy have more 3-bedroom houses?
faq.a: Hough and Glenville, both within a few miles of Little Italy on Cleveland's east side, generally have a larger supply of standalone 3-bedroom rental houses than the immediate Little Italy/University Circle core.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage 3-bedroom homes in Little Italy?
faq.a: Our portfolio is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast-side neighborhoods rather than Little Italy specifically. Every home we manage accepts Section 8 vouchers, so tell our leasing team you need three bedrooms and we'll point you toward comparable current availability.

faq.q: How do I get from Little Italy to Downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: The RTA Red Line heavy rail runs directly from the University Circle station to Downtown's Tower City hub, and the HealthLine bus rapid transit provides a second direct route along Euclid Avenue.
